questions.question: What is the current FDI limit in India for helicopter services requiring DGCA approval?
questions.explanation: India permits 100% FDI in helicopter services requiring DGCA approval as per policy effective 2025-2026. FDI up to 49% is allowed through the automatic route. Beyond 49%, approval under the government route is required. DGCA refers to the Directorate General of Civil Aviation. Earlier FDI caps were less liberal, but current policy separates helicopter services from scheduled and non-scheduled air transport sectors.

questions.question: Who are included in the Labour Force?

options.label: B
options.text: All employed and unemployed, regardless of job-seeking status

options.label: C
options.text: All employed and unemployed actively seeking or available for work

options.label: D
options.text: All employed in formal and informal sectors

answer.label: C
answer.text: All employed and unemployed actively seeking or available for work

questions.explanation: The labour force includes all persons aged 15 years and above who are either employed or unemployed but actively seeking or available for work. The definition is provided by the National Sample Survey Office (NSSO) and International Labour Organization (ILO) guidelines. Students, homemakers, and those not seeking work are excluded from the labour force.

questions.question: The ‘break-even point’ is where _:
questions.explanation: The break-even point (BEP) is the point at which costs or expenses and revenues are equal but there is no net loss or profit, and there is also a “break-even”. There has been no profit or loss, although the opportunity costs have been “paid,” and the capital has received a risk-adjusted, expected return.

questions.question: Which of the following are extreme forms of markets?
questions.explanation: There are two extreme forms of market structure: monopoly and perfect competition. Perfect competition is characterized by multiple buyers and sellers, many products that are similar in nature and, as a result, many alternatives. A monopoly is a market structure in which there is only one manufacturer/seller for a product.

questions.question: Any factor of production can earn an economic rent when its supply is __
questions.explanation: Economic rent is the revenue that can be earned from land or other natural resources for which there is a fixed supply – as economists like to say, the supply is completely inelastic.

questions.question: When the demand for a commodity increases with increase in income, what is such a good called?
questions.explanation: In McConnell’s words, demand for the factors of production is a derived demand derived from the finished goods and services that resources help in production. Whereas demand for a commodity is direct demand, demand for factors is derived demand. It is based on the productivity of the factors.

questions.question: If ___ firm has zero cost or only fixed cost, then the quantity supplied in equilibrium is given from the point where marginal revenue is zero.
questions.explanation: If the cost of the monopoly firm is zero then the quantity supplied in equilibrium is given from the point where marginal revenue will be zero. Whereas perfect competition will supply a given equilibrium quantity by the point where average revenue is zero.

questions.question: To which units can the government pay subsidies?
questions.explanation: Government subsidies can be given to both producers and consumers. Producer subsidies lower costs of production or encourage output; examples include agricultural or industrial subsidies. Consumer subsidies reduce purchase prices; examples include food, fuel, and scholarship subsidies. Both types are implemented in India through schemes such as the Public Distribution System for consumers and Minimum Support Price for producers.

questions.question: What is a moderate, controlled increase in prices after economic contraction called?
questions.explanation: Reflation is a policy-driven increase in prices to counter deflation, implemented through monetary or fiscal measures. It aims to restore the general price level to a long-term trend. Reflation occurs after periods of economic downturn or contraction. Unlike hyperinflation and stagflation, it is deliberate and moderate. Common tools for reflation include lowering interest rates and increasing government spending.

questions.question: Which livestock product holds the highest share in India’s total livestock output value?
questions.explanation: Milk production contributed nearly 67% to India’s total livestock output value as per the 2022-23 economic survey. India became the world’s largest milk producer in 1997. Major dairy development started under Operation Flood launched in 1970. Annual milk production reached 221 million tonnes in 2021-22. Dairy cooperatives are major contributors to countrywide milk collection and distribution.
